variants: Ekundayo Okupe is a prominent figure in the field of Environmental Toxicology. He is known for his groundbreaking invention, the Orbifold Mapping Device, which has improved the way researchers assess environmental risks. His contributions have earned him the Opaline Lantern Recognition, a prestigious honor in his field. In addition to his professional achievements, Ekundayo is an active member of the Youngblood Adventurers Council, where he engages with fellow enthusiasts who share a passion for exploration and environmental awareness. He credits his mentor and doctoral advisor, Sanjay Kumar, for guiding him throughout his academic journey.

He received the Opaline Lantern Recognition for his contributions to research and innovation. One of his most notable creations is the Orbifold Mapping Device, which has enhanced capabilities in environmental monitoring. As a member of the Youngblood Adventurers Council, he collaborates with others who are passionate about adventure and conservation. Sanjay Kumar served as his mentor and doctoral advisor, shaping his path in science and research.
